name: ip-counsel
description: Full IP strategy pipeline: inventory your existing IP, identify unprotected innovations, search prior art, map boundaries between families, draft claims, and produce a filing-ready plan. Each step is a specialized skill — no duplication.

config.instruction: Scan the codebase and existing IP documents. Build a complete inventory of what's already filed, drafted, or documented. Identify innovations in the code that aren't covered yet. Output: ip_inventory, innovation_list, gap_analysis, filing_priority.

Take the innovation_list and gap_analysis from the inventory step. For each unprotected innovation, search prior art. Determine clearance status and recommend claim modifications. Focus on the highest-priority items from filing_priority.
Take the patent_families and ip_inventory from the inventory step. Map boundaries between all existing families. Identify overlaps and conflicts. This runs in parallel with prior art search.

Using the prior art findings and boundary map, draft claims for the highest-priority unprotected innovations. Incorporate differentiation language from prior art. Respect family boundaries. Produce 3 independent + dependent claims per innovation.
Write the patent specification based on the drafted claims. Include all required sections with enablement for every claim element.
Build a filing readiness checklist. Check claims, specification, boundary conflicts, prior art clearance. Output a go/no-go decision with specific action items for anything blocking.
